gatekeeper_definition_criteria.description: Defines the quantitative and qualitative criteria by which large online platforms will be designated as 'gatekeepers' subject to the regulation.
gatekeeper_definition_criteria.salience: Large tech companies (potential gatekeepers) will lobby for high, narrow thresholds and clear, objective criteria to avoid or limit designation. SMEs, startups, and consumer groups will push for broader, more inclusive criteria to ensure effective regulation. Member States might debate the balance between quantitative and qualitative assessments.

scope_core_platform_services.description: Determines which specific digital services offered by gatekeepers, such as online marketplaces or operating systems, fall under the regulatory obligations.
scope_core_platform_services.salience: Gatekeepers will seek to narrow the definition of 'core platform services' to reduce the scope of obligations. Business users and competitors will lobby for a broad interpretation to cover all critical services where gatekeeper power is observed. Different digital sectors (e.g., cloud, voice assistants) will lobby for inclusion or exclusion.

specific_gatekeeper_obligations_prohibit.description: Establishes the concrete 'dos and don'ts' for gatekeepers to ensure contestability and prevent unfair practices, such as self-preferencing or restricting business user access to data.
specific_gatekeeper_obligations_prohibit.salience: Gatekeepers will lobby against prescriptive obligations, arguing for flexibility and innovation. Business users, SMEs, and competitors will demand strong, specific, and enforceable prohibitions to address harms like self-preferencing or lack of data access. Civil society may advocate for specific consumer protection.

enforcement_monitoring_sanctions.description: Defines the institutional framework for enforcement, including the powers of the European Commission, cooperation with national authorities, and the level and type of penalties for non-compliance.
enforcement_monitoring_sanctions.salience: The European Commission will advocate for strong, centralized enforcement powers. Member States may seek to retain a role in enforcement or influence over decisions. Gatekeepers will lobby for due process, appeal mechanisms, and proportionate sanctions.

data_governance_user_tracking_privacy.description: Addresses gatekeepers' practices related to the collection, use, and sharing of end-user data, particularly comprehensive tracking and profiling, and its implications for privacy and data protection.
data_governance_user_tracking_privacy.salience: Gatekeepers will lobby to protect their ability to leverage user data, which is central to many of their business models. Civil society organizations, privacy advocates, and consumer groups will push for stricter controls, opt-in consent for profiling, and greater transparency and user control over data.

impact_innovation_choice_market_efficien.description: Assesses whether the regulation effectively promotes innovation, increases consumer choice, and improves market efficiency without stifling competitive dynamics or European competitiveness.
impact_innovation_choice_market_efficien.salience: Gatekeepers will argue the regulation stifles their ability to innovate and compete globally, leading to reduced consumer benefits. SMEs, startups, and consumer advocates will argue it levels the playing field, fostering genuine innovation and offering more choice and better quality for consumers.

eu_national_competence_fragmentation.description: Addresses the balance of power between EU-level action and national regulatory initiatives, aiming to prevent fragmentation of the Internal Market.
eu_national_competence_fragmentation.salience: Member States with existing or planned national digital regulations may seek to preserve national autonomy or argue for specific national considerations. The Commission and businesses operating cross-border will push for a harmonized, EU-wide approach to reduce compliance burdens and ensure a truly single market.

promotion_societal_values.description: Incorporates broader societal objectives beyond pure economic competition, such as ensuring a level playing field for cultural content and promoting media pluralism.
promotion_societal_values.salience: Media associations, cultural organizations, and certain Member States will advocate for stronger provisions to protect and promote European content and diverse media landscapes. Gatekeepers may argue these objectives are outside the scope of competition regulation or impose disproportionate burdens.
